perturbation.original_text: 1.3.2 "Restricted Activities" means the operation of (i) a wholesale merchandise business that supplies stores of the type described in clause (ii) hereof, or (ii) any store engaged in the retail sale of goods (e.g., toys, health and beauty aids, food, books, party goods, stationery, hardware, housewares, jewelry, hair products, crafts, pet supplies, etc.) where at least eighty percent (80%) of such goods are sold at or below $2.00.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3.2 "Restricted Activities" means the operation of (i) a wholesale merchandise business that supplies stores of the type described in clause (ii) hereof, or (ii) any store engaged in the retail sale of goods (e.g., toys, health and beauty aids, food, books, party goods, stationery, hardware, housewares, jewelry, hair products, crafts, pet supplies, etc.) where at least ninety percent (90%) of such goods are sold at or below $2.00.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the percentage from 80% to 90% for 'Restricted Activities' creates a contradiction. This new definition changes the scope of what is considered a restricted activity. A business selling 85% of its goods below $2.00 would be restricted by the original text but not by the changed text. This affects section 1.1 which describes what activities the selling shareholder can't engage in.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Selling Shareholder agrees, during the period ending five years from the Effective Time ("Restricted Period"), neither Selling Shareholder nor any affiliate of Selling Shareholder shall conduct any of the following activities described in Sections 1.1.1, 1.1.2, and 1.1.3:
perturbation.justification: The change from 80% to 90% in the definition of 'Restricted Activities' directly contradicts section 1.1. As the explanation suggests, a business selling 85% of its goods below $2.00 would have been considered a 'Restricted Activity' under the original text, and thus prohibited by section 1.1. Under the changed text, it is no longer considered a 'Restricted Activity' and therefore no longer prohibited. This creates a direct inconsistency in what activities the selling shareholder is restricted from engaging in.

perturbation.original_text: 1.3.3 A "shopping center" means a group of at least six (6) retail shops organized in a strip or mall configuration (but not necessarily in attached or adjacent buildings) sharing common parking areas and having the same landlord or leasing agent.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3.3 A "shopping center" means a group of at least four (4) retail shops organized in a strip or mall configuration (but not necessarily in attached or adjacent buildings) sharing common parking areas and having the same landlord or leasing agent.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of "shopping center" from 'at least six' to 'at least four' retail shops creates a contradiction. By changing what constitutes a shopping center it can impact the selling shareholder on engaging certain activities listed in section 1.1.3 of this contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1.3 operate any variety, clearance, or close-out retail store in the same shopping center as any retail location of SAI existing as of the Effective Time or, in the case of an existing store of SAI not in a shopping center, within one-quarter mile of such retail location.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of a "shopping center" from 'at least six' to 'at least four' retail shops. This change directly impacts section 1.1.3, which restricts the seller from operating a retail store in the same shopping center as any SAI location. Because the definition of 'shopping center' has been altered, a location with four or five retail shops, which was previously not considered a shopping center under the original definition, now falls under the definition. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the seller is now restricted by section 1.1.3 in situations they were not previously.

perturbation.original_text: 4. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Selling Shareholder shall not, without the prior written approval of Dollar Tree, at any time copy, reproduce, disclose or divulge to any third party, use or permit others to use any confidential information of SAI or Dollar Tree (including financial information, sources of supplies and materials, prospective and existing expansion programs, joint ventures and affiliate agreements, business systems and marketing methods, and all business affairs). During the Restricted Period, Selling Shareholder shall keep secret and retain in strictest confidence, and shall not use for the benefit of Selling Shareholder, Selling Shareholder's affiliate, or others, all confidential matters of the Business or of Dollar Tree, including trade secrets, details of supplier contracts, lease provisions and terms, operational methods and marketing plans or strategies. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing herein contained shall preclude Selling Shareholder from using the confidential information described in this Section 4 in the operation of or in the leasing of any real property or the operation of any business in connection with a business not coming within the definition of Restricted Activities or for other personal use, so long as such use does not conflict with any provision of this Agreement. This Section 4 shall not apply if (a) such information is already known to such party or to others not bound by a duty of confidentiality or such information is or becomes publicly available through no fault of Selling Shareholder or Selling Shareholder's Affiliates, (b) the use of such information is necessary or appropriate in making any filing or obtaining any consent or approval required for the consummation of the closing under the Merger Agreement, or (c) the furnishing or use of such information is required by legal proceedings.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Selling Shareholder shall not, without the prior written approval of Dollar Tree, at any time copy, reproduce, disclose or divulge to any third party, use or permit others to use any confidential information of SAI or Dollar Tree (including financial information, sources of supplies and materials, prospective and existing expansion programs, joint ventures and affiliate agreements, business systems and marketing methods, and all business affairs). Selling Shareholder shall keep secret and retain in strictest confidence, and shall not use for the benefit of Selling Shareholder, Selling Shareholder's affiliate, or others, all confidential matters of the Business or of Dollar Tree, including trade secrets, details of supplier contracts, lease provisions and terms, operational methods and marketing plans or strategies. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Selling Shareholder is allowed to use the confidential information described in this Section 4 in the operation of or in the leasing of any real property or the operation of any business. This Section 4 shall not apply if (a) such information is already known to such party or to others not bound by a duty of confidentiality or such information is or becomes publicly available through no fault of Selling Shareholder or Selling Shareholder's Affiliates, (b) the use of such information is necessary or appropriate in making any filing or obtaining any consent or approval required for the consummation of the closing under the Merger Agreement, or (c) the furnishing or use of such information is required by legal proceedings.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'During the Restricted Period' and changing 'so long as such use does not conflict with any provision of this Agreement' to a unconditional statement, I'm contradicting this section within itself. Section 4 begins by strictly limiting the use of confidential information, but then the edited version allows the Selling Shareholder to freely use it, creating in-text contradiction. The original text had a constraint by saying only in the operation of any business not coming within the definition of Restricted Activities, but it has been removed in the changed text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. NON-DISCLOSURE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Selling Shareholder shall not, without the prior written approval of Dollar Tree, at any time copy, reproduce, disclose or divulge to any third party, use or permit others to use any confidential information of SAI or Dollar Tree (including financial information, sources of supplies and materials, prospective and existing expansion programs, joint ventures and affiliate agreements, business systems and marketing methods, and all business affairs). Selling Shareholder shall keep secret and retain in strictest confidence, and shall not use for the benefit of Selling Shareholder, Selling Shareholder's affiliate, or others, all confidential matters of the Business or of Dollar Tree, including trade secrets, details of supplier contracts, lease provisions and terms, operational methods and marketing plans or strategies. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Selling Shareholder is allowed to use the confidential information described in this Section 4 in the operation of or in the leasing of any real property or the operation of any business. This Section 4 shall not apply if (a) such information is already known to such party or to others not bound by a duty of confidentiality or such information is or becomes publicly available through no fault of Selling Shareholder or Selling Shareholder's Affiliates, (b) the use of such information is necessary or appropriate in making any filing or obtaining any consent or approval required for the consummation of the closing under the Merger Agreement, or (c) the furnishing or use of such information is required by legal proceedings.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'During the Restricted Period' and changes the conditional clause 'so long as such use does not conflict with any provision of this Agreement' to an unconditional statement: 'Selling Shareholder is allowed to use the confidential information described in this Section 4 in the operation of or in the leasing of any real property or the operation of any business.' This creates a direct conflict with the initial clause of Section 4, which restricts the use of confidential information. Now, the same section both prohibits and permits the use of confidential information without restriction, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2. As used herein, the term "affiliate" with respect to any person shall mean any person or entity which controls such person, which that person controls, or which is under common control with that person. In the case of Selling Shareholder, the term "affiliate" shall include the spouse, sibling, or lineal descendant or ancestor of Selling Shareholder, a trust for the benefit of any of the preceding, or any corporation, partnership, joint venture, or other entity which the Selling Shareholder, the spouse, sibling, or lineal descendant or ancestor of Selling Shareholder, or a trust for the benefit of any of the preceding, controls. For purposes of the preceding sentences, the term "control" means the power, direct or indirect through one or more intermediaries, to direct or cause to the direction of the management and policies of a person or entity through voting securities, contract, or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2. As used herein, the term "affiliate" with respect to any person shall mean any person or entity which has a business relationship with such person. In the case of Selling Shareholder, the term "affiliate" shall include any entity in which the Selling Shareholder has invested. For purposes of the preceding sentences, the term "control" means the influence, direct or indirect through one or more intermediaries, to impact the policies of a person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'affiliate' to a more vague 'business relationship' and 'any entity in which the Selling Shareholder has invested' creates a conflict with section 1.1 that restricts affiliates. A business relationship is too vague and is not defined. The original text specified control to mean power to direct management via voting securities etc. It is also contradicting section 6.1 as the Selling Shareholder is also restricted through a shareholder from their affiliates, the definition of affiliate has been changed but the obligation has not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Selling Shareholder agrees, during the period ending five years from the Effective Time ("Restricted Period"), neither Selling Shareholder nor any affiliate of Selling Shareholder shall conduct any of the following activities described in Sections 1.1.1, 1.1.2, and 1.1.3:
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the definition of "affiliate" to include any entity with a "business relationship" or in which the Selling Shareholder has invested. This contradicts section 1.1, which restricts the activities of the Selling Shareholder and its affiliates. The original definition was more specific and control-based, while the new definition is much more vague, meaning a much wider range of entities are now restricted by section 1.1. Also the changed text modifies the term 'control' to influence instead of power, which now contradicts with the Selling Shareholder's obligations due to their affiliates in section 6.1

perturbation.original_text: 15. GOVERNING LAW. While Selling Shareholder is a resident of California, the Agreement shall be governed by the internal laws of the State of California, and the Courts of the State of California and the United States District Court for the Eastern District of California shall be the exclusive courts of jurisdiction and venue for any litigation, special proceeding or other proceeding as between the parties that may be brought, or arise out of, in connection with, or by reason of this Agreement. If Selling Shareholder ceases to be a resident of California, immediately upon Selling Shareholder's change of residency, this Agreement shall be governed by the internal ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Non-Competition Agreement -- Page 7 <PAGE> laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, and the Circuit Court of the City of Norfolk and the United States District Court, Eastern District of Virginia, Norfolk Division, shall be the exclusive courts of jurisdiction and venue for any litigation, special proceeding or other proceeding as between the parties that may be brought, or arise out of, in connection with, or by reason of this Agreement. The parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of such courts.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. GOVERNING LAW. While Selling Shareholder is a resident of California, the Agreement shall be governed by the internal laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, and the Courts of the State of California and the United States District Court for the Eastern District of California shall be the exclusive courts of jurisdiction and venue for any litigation, special proceeding or other proceeding as between the parties that may be brought, or arise out of, in connection with, or by reason of this Agreement. If Selling Shareholder ceases to be a resident of California, immediately upon Selling Shareholder's change of residency, this Agreement shall be governed by the internal ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Non-Competition Agreement -- Page 7 <PAGE> laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, and the Circuit Court of the City of Norfolk and the United States District Court, Eastern District of Virginia, Norfolk Division, shall be the exclusive courts of jurisdiction and venue for any litigation, special proceeding or other proceeding as between the parties that may be brought, or arise out of, in connection with, or by reason of this Agreement. The parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of such courts.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'the internal laws of the State of California' to 'the internal laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia' creates an in-text contradiction because now there is only one governing law (Commonwealth of Virginia), creating uncertainty of which law should be used for any litigation, depending if the Selling Shareholder is currently a resident of California.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. GOVERNING LAW. While Selling Shareholder is a resident of California, the Agreement shall be governed by the internal laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, and the Courts of the State of California and the United States District Court for the Eastern District of California shall be the exclusive courts of jurisdiction and venue for any litigation, special proceeding or other proceeding as between the parties that may be brought, or arise out of, in connection with, or by reason of this Agreement. If Selling Shareholder ceases to be a resident of California, immediately upon Selling Shareholder's change of residency, this Agreement shall be governed by the internal ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Non-Competition Agreement -- Page 7 <PAGE> laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, and the Circuit Court of the City of Norfolk and the United States District Court, Eastern District of Virginia, Norfolk Division, shall be the exclusive courts of jurisdiction and venue for any litigation, special proceeding or other proceeding as between the parties that may be brought, or arise out of, in connection with, or by reason of this Agreement. The parties hereby consent to the jurisdiction of such courts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law for when the Selling Shareholder is a resident of California from 'the internal laws of the State of California' to 'the internal laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia'. This creates a direct conflict within the same section, as it now states that the agreement is governed by the laws of Virginia regardless of the Selling Shareholder's residency. The Contradicted Text section is the same section as the Changed Text, the change introduces an inconsistency because the agreement now dictates different governing laws based on the same condition, creating uncertainty and a clear in-text contradiction as defined by the provided 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
